kanban_v2_schema.signature: KanbanCard adds: created_by (str), locked_by_run_id (str | None FK), completed_by_run_id (str | None FK), agent_status (Literal['idle','claimed','running','blocked','completed']), metadata (str | None, json), agent_run_id (str | None FK). KanbanColumn adds: is_autopilot (bool, default false)
kanban_v2_schema.description: Schema v2 migration extending existing Kanban tables. Alembic migration includes backfill: existing cards get created_by='human' and agent_status='idle'. SQLite canonical; board.json becomes read-only projection.

kanban_tools.signature: @tool list_cards(column: str | None) -> [KanbanCard]; @tool get_card(id: str) -> KanbanCard; @tool add_card(column: str, title: str, description: str, labels: [str]) -> KanbanCard; @tool move_card(id: str, to_column: str, rev: int) -> KanbanCard; @tool update_card(id: str, fields: dict, rev: int) -> KanbanCard; @tool comment_card(id: str, comment: str) -> None
kanban_tools.description: In-process SDK @tool functions exposing kanban CRUD to agents. All writes route through kanban_service.move_card with optimistic concurrency (per-card rev numbers). Card description is delimited as <untrusted>...</untrusted> before going into any agent prompt to mitigate prompt injection.

kanban_service_extensions.signature: def move_card(id, to_column, rev) -> Card; def update_card(id, fields, rev) -> Card; def lock_card(id, run_id) -> bool; def unlock_card(id, run_id) -> None
kanban_service_extensions.description: Extended kanban_service methods enforcing SQL transactions, re-read before write, rev-bump on update, locked_by_run_id check, and board.json regeneration (read-only projection) after each mutation.

autopilot_skill.description: New skill file. Polls autopilot-flagged columns; for each unclaimed card, claims via lock, dispatches card-to-skill route (bug -> bug-fix, feature -> add-set, default -> quick), passes card id and description as skill args, monitors result, marks card completed or moves to 'Blocked' on failure after N=3 retries.

autopilot_worker.signature: app/agents/autopilot_worker.py: async def run_autopilot_loop(session_manager, kanban_service, stop_event)
autopilot_worker.description: Lifespan-managed async poller (60s interval configurable). Checks is_autopilot columns for unclaimed cards. For each card, dispatches a /rapid:autopilot run through SessionManager. Respects per-project concurrency cap (reuses semaphore). Dies gracefully on stop_event.

card_to_skill_routing.description: Label-based routing: 'bug' -> /rapid:bug-fix; 'feature' -> /rapid:add-set; 'chore' or default -> /rapid:quick. Configurable via kanban_service.route_config in SQLite.

retry_and_blocked_policy.signature: per-card retries limited to N=3 (configurable); card-level timeout configurable; on failure moves card to 'Blocked' column and clears locked_by_run_id
retry_and_blocked_policy.description: Prevents runaway loops. 'Blocked' / 'Needs Review' column halts retries. UI surfaces 'this card failed 3 times' badge.

commit_trailer_traceability.signature: Every git commit produced during an autopilot run appends: Autopilot-Card-Id: <card_id>\nAutopilot-Run-Id: <run_id>
commit_trailer_traceability.description: Git trailers traceable back to originating kanban card. Enables /rapid:status-style views of agent-produced work.

ui_agent_badges.signature: <KanbanCard /> component renders badges: 'Agent claimed' (locked_by_run_id set), 'Agent created' (created_by starts with 'agent:'), 'Agent completed' (completed_by_run_id set)
ui_agent_badges.description: Visual indication on kanban cards showing agent involvement. 'Run with RAPID' per-card action button routes through the same dispatch flow.

create_sdk_mcp_server_integration.description: We register kanban @tools through this integration so agent runs have kanban access

AgentSessionManager.description: Autopilot worker dispatches runs via session manager; reuses per-project semaphore

build_sdk_options.description: Our MCP server must be registered into ClaudeAgentOptions by build_sdk_options; we provide the tool list and server name

agent_run_model.description: kanban_card.agent_run_id is an FK to agent_runs.id; completed_by_run_id too

run_id_correlation.description: Used for commit trailers and lock ownership (locked_by_run_id)

per_skill_permission_policy.description: We add an 'autopilot' entry to the foundation's permission policy registry (default mode + restricted Bash allowlist)

board_json_is_read_only_projection.description: No agent or code path writes to board.json directly. It is regenerated from SQLite after every mutation. Tests verify: mutate kanban card -> board.json on disk matches SQLite state.

optimistic_concurrency_via_rev.description: move_card and update_card require the caller's current rev; writes fail with StaleRevError if rev mismatches. Prevents torn writes when human and agent mutate concurrently.

max_3_retries_per_card.description: After 3 failed autopilot attempts, card moves to 'Blocked' column and autopilot stops retrying. 'Needs Review' column likewise halts. Prevents runaway loops and token burn.

card_description_untrusted_wrapped.description: Every kanban card description is wrapped as <untrusted>...</untrusted> before inclusion in any agent prompt. System prompt warns the agent to treat content as untrusted input.

autopilot_worker_respects_concurrency_ca.description: The autopilot worker does NOT bypass the per-project semaphore. If the cap is saturated, worker backs off and retries on next poll cycle.

locked_by_run_id_prevents_double_claim.description: Lock acquisition (SQL UPDATE WHERE locked_by_run_id IS NULL) is atomic. A card that is already claimed cannot be claimed a second time in the same poll cycle.

commit_trailer_present_on_every_autopilo.description: Every git commit authored during an autopilot run has Autopilot-Card-Id and Autopilot-Run-Id trailers. Verified by PreToolUse hook on Bash git commit.
